purpose: Schema for collecting hackathon winners, GitHub/open-source apps, indie AI products, and viral product examples before updating Concept Strategist rules.

itemShape.name: product or project name
itemShape.sourceCategory: hackathon_winner | github_oss | indie_product | product_launch | research_signal | platform_product
audience: general_user | office_worker | developer | ai_power_user | creator | domain_expert | civic_user | student | founder
itemShape.surfaceTheme: work | education | creativity | civic | security | AI literacy | developer_tool | agent_workflow | entertainment | sports | other
itemShape.surfacePattern: daily_utility | learning_explainer | playful_game | decision_helper | social_civic_tool | event_companion | creative_assistant | work_simplifier | developer_tool | other
itemShape.aiMechanismPattern: personalized_reasoning | multi_source_synthesis | agentic_workflow | simulation | evaluation_scoring | trust_boundary | adaptive_explainer | workflow_generation | code_generation | computer_use | other
ideaMoves: metaphor | diagnosis | transformation | comparison | simulation | learning_package | personalization | radar | failure_analysis | self_relevance | automation | marketplace | observability
itemShape.interestingMechanic: the interaction, mechanism, or structural trick worth learning from
itemShape.transferableRule: rule that Concept Strategist can reuse
itemShape.notes: short caveat or next research need

analysisAxes: Does the surface theme make sense to non-engineers in five seconds?
Is there an AI-native mechanism underneath the simple surface?
Does the user provide their own input or context?
Does the product create visible state change instead of only summarizing?
Is there an aha moment, failure moment, or judgment moment?
Can the idea be made as a small one-screen artifact?
